DR Format updated and subentry 8 added
SD: BIB updated in every Subent.
Pure sample of 254g-Es.
Silicon surface barrier alpha spectrometer
The 60-CO produced was assayed by measuring the
1332-keV gamma with a Ge(Li) detector.
No information about source of uncertainties
Neutron flux corrected for decay and burnup in
monitor
Pure sample of 254g-Es.
Silicon surface barrier alpha spectrometer
No information about source of uncertainties
Neutron flux corrected for decay and burnup in
monitor
Solid state track detector
An especially purified sample of 254g-Es free of
californium was deposited onto a thin nickel foil and
placed in contact with 0.2-mm thick mica. A deposit of
233U was used to monitor the neutron flux, and a
similar assembly was placed back-to-back to the
einsteinium sample for that purpose.
Techniques of solid-state track detection:
R.L.Fleischer+, Nuclear Tracks in Solids, University of
California Press, Berkeley, California (1975).
The tracks were examined and counted under a microscope
at ~200-fold magnification. A number of replicate runs
were carried out to enhance the statistics of the
measurement.
No information about source of uncertainties
from the text on p.301 of NSE,90,298,1985
